text: Secure web development is essential for protecting user data and maintaining trust. HTTPS encrypts communication between users and websites, preventing hackers from intercepting sensitive information. Authentication ensures only authorised users can access certain features, often through passwords or multi-factor methods. Input validation is another key practice—it checks user data on the server side to block malicious code injections. Common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting can be avoided by properly sanitising inputs and using prepared statements. Regular security updates and testing are equally important. By prioritising these measures, developers can build safer web applications that defend against attacks.
